label: Decoder-only causal transformers

node.label: Decoder-only causal transformers
node.definition: A decoder-only transformer predicts each next token from a causally masked context, enabling scalable autoregressive pretraining, continuation, and instruction following.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Decoder-only causal transformers is to connect Transformers, Causal, masked & structured masking to downstream capabilities including Retrieval-augmented generation, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Program synthesis & code search.
examples: substrate for Program synthesis & code search
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Transformers, Causal, masked & structured masking.

dependencies.rationale: Decoder-only causal transformers extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
Decoder-only causal transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Causal, masked & structured masking.
Decoder-only causal transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Autoregressive decoding.
Decoder-only causal transformers is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Autoregressive / next-step prediction.
